the_problem: Re-enrollment in workflows can cause automated logic to overwrite manual adjustments. If re-enrollment is enabled on a workflow that sets property values, any manual override performed by a user will be overwritten when the record next meets the re-enrollment criteria.
how_it_manifests: Users (e.g., CSMs or sales reps) manually override a property (such as risk status or lead score), only for it to be automatically reverted upon the next workflow trigger
Users report that the system is 'broken' or ignoring their manual updates
when_to_surface_this: Any workflow design where an action sets a property value AND re-enrollment is enabled
Workflows managing properties that are also user-facing or actively edited by team members
mitigation_options: Disable re-enrollment on the workflow if automated updates should only occur once
Implement an 'override protection' boolean property that workflows check in their enrollment criteria before updating values
Educate users and document that manual edits on workflow-managed fields are temporary and subject to automated reset
